title: NOC 63210 – Hairstylists and Barbers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 63210 (Hairstylists and Barbers) are essential to the personal service sector in Canada. This TEER 3 occupation is highly mobile and offers incredible entrepreneurial opportunities. Many provinces offer specialized trade streams for hairstylists, recognizing their importance to the local economy. GTR Immigration helps stylists bring their creative flair to Canada while securing their future.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level3Major Group63 – Occupations in personal serviceSub-Major Group632 – Occupations in personal serviceMinor Group6321 – Hairstylists and barbersNOC Code63210

duties: Suggest hair styles compatible with client's physical features.
Cut, trim, taper, curl, wave, perm and style hair.
Apply bleach, tints, dyes or rinses to color, frost or streak hair.
Analyze hair and scalp condition and provide basic treatment or advice.
Clean and style wigs and hairpieces.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a two- or three-year apprenticeship program OR completion of a hairstyling program is mandatory.
Trade certification is compulsory in many provinces (e.g., ON, AB, QC).
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. The constant need for grooming services ensures a baseline of job security.
labour_market_demand: The grooming and beauty industry is resilient. Demand is high for specialized stylists (colorists, barbering) in urban centers. Self-employment is very common in this NOC.
High Self-Employment: Over 40% of stylists in Canada work for themselves.
Professional Standards: High barrier to entry ensures quality and high service fees.
Creative Hubs: Opportunities in film, fashion, and high-end salons.
pathways: Self-Employed Persons Program: For world-class or highly independent stylists.
Express Entry (CEC): For those working in established salons.
Provincial Nominee Program (PNP): Trade-specific draws in provinces like Alberta.
